prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveSeasonal', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.38) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. NaiveSeasonal dominates trend tracking while ETS leads in seasonality capture, with FT_catboost specializing in late horizons. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "ARIMA", "DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_svr", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "THETA", "CWT_svr", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3834,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.38) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. NaiveSeasonal dominates trend tracking while ETS leads in seasonality capture, with FT_catboost specializing in late horizons. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk5", "ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so ridge stacking methods are viable and show strong performance (RMSE~67.47 vs current best 69.96). Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 significantly outperforms current candidates. Also adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 which has good stability (SMAPE_std=0.0038) and competitive RMSE (68.35). Current set already has diversity with selection+weighted methods, so no removals needed.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows stacking advantage", "added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 for stability and competitive RMSE"], "when_good": "When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes more viable; currently at n_windows=3 it's still beneficial due to regularization benefits."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.38) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture cyclic patterns very differently. NaiveSeasonal dominates trend tracking while ETS leads in seasonality capture, with FT_catboost specializing in late horizons. Given both high disagreement and high seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon model variation, favoring selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.3834 shows significant gap between best (ETS: 69.75) and worst models (catboost: 95.89), justifying top-k approaches with shrinkage. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.96 and model disagreement. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.25 on inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions from trend_champions (NaiveSeasonal) and seasonality_champions (ETS).

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so ridge stacking methods are viable and show strong performance (RMSE~67.47 vs current best 69.96). Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 significantly outperforms current candidates. Also adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 which has good stability (SMAPE_std=0.0038) and competitive RMSE (68.35). Current set already has diversity with selection+weighted methods, so no removals needed.
changes: added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows stacking advantage
added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 for stability and competitive RMSE
statistician.when_good: When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes more viable; currently at n_windows=3 it's still beneficial due to regularization benefits.
